revision.text:
[[ja:茶臼塚古墳 (柏原市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Rectangular Kofun|ja|長方形墳|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rectangular Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |name = Chausuzuka Kofun |image = | part_of = {{ill|Matsuokayama Kofun Group|qid=Q60986182|1=Matsuokayama Kofun Group}} |location = {{ill|Kashiwara|qid=Q873583|1=Kashiwara}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.56925|N|135.64430555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = Rectangular mound |area = 17m (east-west) × 22m (north-south) |built = {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|qid=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}} |Burial Facility = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |Interred Persons = |burial goods = {{ill|Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, jasper arm ornaments, and more |史跡 = None |有形文化財 = excavations (Kashiwara City Designated designation1) |Special Notes = |architectural_styles = {{ill|Q80793203|qid=Q80793203|1=Rectangular Kofun}} }} '''Chausuzuka Kofun''' (茶臼塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kashiwara|en|Kashiwara|qid=Q873583|lt=Kashiwara, Osaka|1=Kashiwara}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan. The Kofun is rectangular in type. Although not Historic Site1 as a historic site, its excavated burial goods are recognized as Kashiwara City Designated Historic Site. The kofun is situated west of the {{ill|Matsuokayama Kofun|en|Matsuokayama Kofun|qid=Q11530088|lt=Matsuokayama Kofun|1=Matsuokayama Kofun}} (a National Historic Site) and is almost adjacent to it. Local residents discovered the stone chamber in 1985, prompting immediate archaeological investigations. excavations include numerous jasper arm ornaments and a {{ill|triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}. == Overview == In December 1984, the stone chamber of the kofun was discovered by the property owner while installing a water tank in an orchard. Many relics, including bronze mirrors and stone tools, were unearthed. Emergency investigations were carried out by the Kashiwara City Board of Education, followed by a full excavation in the following year to confirm the mound's type, size, and structure. Initially believed to be a circular mound with a diameter of 20 meters, partial excavations revealed it to be a rectangular mound. === Internal burial === The burial facility is a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} constructed with andesite slab stones stacked vertically. The chamber measures 620 cm in length, 100 cm in width, and 170 cm in height. The southern half of the chamber has collapsed significantly, with the upper eastern wall also partially collapsed. However, the western and northern walls remain well-preserved. A clay floor where a wooden coffin was placed was identified, but no traces of the wooden coffin itself remain. === Mound burial === The mound consists of an earthen core covered with andesite slabs similar to those used in the stone chamber. The outer layer features vertically stacked slab stones approximately 30 cm × 20 cm × 5 cm in size, typeing almost vertical walls. Partial excavations at four sites suggest that the mound is a two-tiered rectangular structure measuring approximately 17 meters east-west, 22 meters north-south, and 3 meters in height. === burial goods === burial goods from the stone chamber include: * {{ill|Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} (1 piece) * Four-deity mirror (1 piece) * Jasper arm ornaments: ** Hoe-shaped pendants (6 pieces) ** Wheel-shaped pendants (8 pieces) ** Stone bracelets (41 pieces) * Small knives (2 pieces) * Iron axe (1 piece) * Sickle (1 piece) * Adzes (2 pieces) The jasper arm ornaments, totaling 55 items, represent an unusually large collection for a small kofun. These burial goods are Historic Site1 as Kashiwara City Historic Site and are housed at the Kashiwara City Historical Museum. 茶臼塚古墳 (柏原市) 出土 三角縁神獣鏡.JPG|Triangular-rimmed Mirror with Deities and Beasts
{{small|Exhibited at Kashiwara City Historical Museum (same for other images).|qq=}} 茶臼塚古墳 (柏原市) 出土 四獣鏡.JPG|Four-Beast Mirror 茶臼塚古墳 (柏原市) 出土 碧玉製腕飾り (鍬形石).JPG|Jade Arm Ornament (Kuwagata-shaped Stone) 茶臼塚古墳 (柏原市) 出土 碧玉製腕飾り (石釧)・碧玉製腕飾り (車輪石).JPG|Jade Arm Ornaments (Ishikazari and Wheel-shaped Stone) 茶臼塚古墳 (柏原市) 出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical Haniwa == Identity of the Deceased == Due to the circumstances of the discovery, it is uncertain whether all original grave goods were recovered. However, evidence suggests that any missing items were minimal. Based on the predominance of stone arm ornaments, the deceased is believed to have held ritual or religious authority. The close proximity of the kofun to the {{ill|Matsuokayama Kofun|en|Matsuokayama Kofun|qid=Q11530088|lt=Matsuokayama Kofun|1=Matsuokayama Kofun}} suggests that the deceased may have played a supporting role to the leadership buried in the Matsugaokayama Kofun. == Historic Site == === Kashiwara City Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** excavations from Chausuzuka Kofun (archaeological materials), stored at the Kashiwara City Historical Museum, Historic Site1 on August 21, 2008: *** Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (1 piece) *** Four-deity mirror (1 piece) *** Hoe-shaped pendants (6 pieces) *** Wheel-shaped pendants (8 pieces) *** Stone bracelets (41 pieces) *** Small knives (2 pieces) *** Iron axe (1 piece) *** Sickle (1 piece) *** Arrowheads (2 pieces) *** Cylindrical haniwa (1 piece) == References == {{reflist|qq=}} == Bibliography == * Ishida, Toshiharu. "Chausuzuka Kofun, Osaka." _Quarterly Archaeology No. 40_, Yoshikawa Kobunkan, 1992, pp. 72-73. * {{ill|Shiraishi Taichiro|ja|白石太一郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraishi Taichiro|qq=}}. "Pit-style Stone Chamber of Chausuzuka Kofun, Osaka." _Archaeological Perspectives on Yamato_, Aoki Shoten, 2009, pp. 75-77. == See Also == * {{ill|Matsugaokayama Kofun Group|ja|松岳山古墳群|1=Matsuokayama Kofun Group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsugaokayama Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuokayama Kofun Group|qq=}} * {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.isekiwalker.com/iseki/111004/ Chausuzuka Kofun: Iseki Walker] {{Kofun navbar|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|茶臼塚古墳 (柏原市)|version=100337174|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:茶臼塚古墳 (柏原市)]] {{Commons category|Chausuzuka Kofun (Kashiwara)}} {{wikidata link|Q11618007}} [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:柏原市の歴史]] [[Category:長方形墳]]
